This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Mary Merritt Crawford and Family papers
Crawford, Mary M. (Mary Merritt), 1884-
41-5-619
10.2 cubic feet. 10.2 cubic feet.
2 audiocassettes. 2 audiocassettes.
1903-1964. Includes campaign material and reports to the Cornell Alumni Corporation (1932-37) upon completion of each of her terms as alumni trustee; tape recordings and a partial transcript of interviews with Dr. Crawford (1962, 1964) concerning her life as a Cornell co-ed, her career as a doctor, and her service on the Board of Trustees. Also, correspondence, notes, photographs, and printed material concerning alumni affairs, including lists of Cornell women graduates (1873-88) and biographical and bibliographical data compiled by Bessie Bell DeWitt (Mrs. Willard Beahan) and printed material compiled by Simon Henry Gage, Professor of Histology and Embryology, on Susanna Phelps Gage ('80), Mary Hannah Gage-Day, M.D., Theobald Smith ('81), Professor of Histology and Embryology, and the first faculty of the Veterinary College. Medical hood worn by Dr. Crawford as a Cornell Trustee. Papers of Mary Merritt Crawford include student scrapbook, 1900-1904; diaries; photographs, including spring day; and extensive correspondence, 1887-1973, between Mary and other family members especially her parents; her sisters, Charlotte, Lucy, and Caroline; her husband Edward Schuster; and her daughter Mary Schuster Jaffe. Of particular interest are the letters she wrote to her family while she was serving in the Ambulance Corps in France during World War I. Many have typescript copies. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 2 11 ======= 2 11 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2100916
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Fredrika Loew
Date completed:
March 2018
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, March 2018
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, September 2018
RELATED MATERIALS
RELATED MATERIALS
NOTES
Some letters by Mary Merritt Crawford written while she was a surgeon at the American Ambulance Hospital in France were published in Vol. I, No. 2-5 (1915-16) of the Cornell Women's Review.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Mary Merritt Crawford
Series II. Caroline Cecilia Crawford Wolff
Series III. Charlotte Holmes Crawford
Series IV. Sarah Crawford
Series V. Lucinda Crawford
Series VI. Mary Crawford Schuster Jaffe
Series VII. Crawford Family Member Papers
Series VIII. Crawford Family photographs
Cite As:
Mary Merritt Crawford Papers, #41-5-619. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Physician, Cornell University alumni trustee. Mary Merritt Crawford graduated from Cornell in 1904, and received her M.D. in 1907. She was later elected alumni trustee.
Scope and content
Includes Crawford family photographs and correspondence. Mary Merritt Crawford materials include: campaign material and reports to the Cornell Alumni Corporation (1932-37) upon completion of each of her terms as alumni trustee; tape recordings and a partial transcript of interviews with Dr. Crawford (1962, 1964) concerning her life as a Cornell co-ed, her career as a doctor, and her service on the Board of Trustees. Also, correspondence, notes, photographs, and printed material concerning alumni affairs, including lists of Cornell women graduates (1873-88) and biographical and bibliographical data compiled by Bessie Bell DeWitt (Mrs. Willard Beahan) and printed material compiled by Simon Henry Gage, Professor of Histology and Embryology, on Susanna Phelps Gage ('80), Mary Hannah Gage-Day, M.D., Theobald Smith ('81), Professor of Histology and Embryology, and the first faculty of the Veterinary College. Medical hood worn by Dr. Crawford as a Cornell Trustee.
Scope and content
Papers of Mary Merritt Crawford include student scrapbook, 1900-1904; diaries; photographs, including spring day; and extensive correspondence, 1887-1973, between Mary and other family members especially her parents; her sisters, Charlotte, Lucy, and Caroline; her husband Edward Schuster; and her daughter Mary Schuster Jaffe. Of particular interest are the letters she wrote to her family while she was serving in the Ambulance Corps in France during World War I. Many have typescript copies.
Diaries. Scrapbooks. Photographs. Physicians. World War, 1914-1918 -- Personal narratives, American. Ambulance service -- France. World War, 1914-1918 -- Medical care -- France. Women physicians. Women college students. DeWitt, Bessie Bell. Gage, Simon Henry, 1851-1944. Gage, Susanna Phelps. Gage-Day, Mary Hannah. Smith, Theobald, 1859-1934. Crawford, Charlotte. Crawford, Lucy. Schuster, Edward. Jaffe, Mary Schuster. Wolff, Caroline Crawford. Schuster, Mary Merritt Crawford, 1884-1972. Cornell Alumni Corporation Cornell University. Board of Trustees Cornell University -- : Alumni and alumnae. Cornell University -- : Students. American Hospital of Paris
